learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 7 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 5 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils applied a consistent theme, transitions, and animations to their 'About Me' presentation.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils add internal and external URLs to their presentation.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know where to locate the options for sound and video, understand the different audio and video options available within PowerPoint, and can add appropriate sound and/or video content to their 'About Me' presentation.
assessment_criteria: Each student will locate and use the audio or video insertion options in PowerPoint.
Each student will insert at least one piece of appropriate sound or video content into their presentation.
Each student will test their inserted media plays correctly in slideshow mode.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual addition of appropriate audio and/or video content to pupils' 'About Me' PowerPoint, followed by testing playback in slideshow mode.

key_vocabulary.definition: A recorded voice track added to a slide or presentation.
To insert a file (e.g. audio or video) directly into a slide; everyday meaning relates to fixing something firmly in place.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils with sourcing or recording appropriate audio (e.g. a short narration clip) if they do not have a suitable existing file.
accessibility: Captioned or transcript-supported video sources offered where relevant

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to open their PowerPoint and note, on mini-whiteboards, which tab they used to apply their theme last lesson.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Recall and note the Design tab, and one transition or animation they applied.

check_for_understanding.question: What effect does a transition have, compared to an animation?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils correctly distinguish the two; otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Show where the audio and video insertion options are located, and explain the different options available (inserting a video file, recording narration, inserting an audio clip).
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and identify which option they think best suits their own presentation.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model inserting a short video clip on one slide and recording a brief narration on another, then play both back to check they work in slideshow mode.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and note the steps needed to insert and test each type of media.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the task of adding appropriate sound and/or video to their presentation; circulate with least-help-first prompts, including for pupils choosing to record their own narration.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Insert appropriate sound and/or video content into their 'About Me' presentation and test it plays correctly.

check_for_understanding.question: Have you tested your inserted media plays correctly in slideshow mode?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils whose media fails to play are supported individually before moving on.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Facilitate a paired listen-and-check; cold-call two pairs to share what media they added and why.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Swap with a partner, listen to/watch each other's inserted media, and give one piece of feedback.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: what sound or video did you add, and to which slide?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils who have not yet added any media are flagged for a brief catch-up prompt at the start of next lesson.
